Sing 2 (2021)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $162,790,990Details
International Box Office $241,743,598
Worldwide Box Office $404,534,588
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,503,895
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,258,688
Total $10,762,583
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Buster has turned the New Moon Theater into a local hit, but Buster has his eyes on a bigger prize: Debuting a new show at the Crystal Tower Theater in glamorous Redshore City. But with no connections, Buster and his cast — including harried mother pig Rosita, rocker porcupine Ash, earnest gorilla Johnny, shy elephant Meena and, of course, porcine provocateur Gunter — have to sneak their way into the world-famous Crystal Entertainment offices, run by ruthless mogul wolf Jimmy Crystal.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$22,326,230 (13.7% of total gross)
Legs:7.29 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$85,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,892 opening theaters/3,892 max. theaters, 11.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $187,973,454

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 22nd, 2021 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: December 1st, 2021 (Wide), released as Cantar! 2 (Portugal)
December 2nd, 2021 (Wide), released as Énekelj! 2. (Hungary)
December 3rd, 2021 (Wide) (Israel)
December 9th, 2021 (Wide) (Egypt)
December 9th, 2021 (Wide) (Cyprus)
Video Release: January 7th, 2022 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some rude material and mild peril/violence.
(Rating bulletin 2673 (cert #53137), 3/24/2021)

Franchise: Sing

Source:Original Screenplay

Production Method:Digital Animation

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Buster has turned the New Moon Theater into a local hit, but Buster has his eyes on a bigger prize: Debuting a new show at the Crystal Tower Theater in glamorous Redshore City. But with no connections, Buster and his cast — including harried mother pig Rosita, rocker porcupine Ash, earnest gorilla Johnny, shy elephant Meena and, of course, porcine provocateur Gunter — have to sneak their way into the world-famous Crystal Entertainment offices, run by ruthless mogul wolf Jimmy Crystal.

Theater counts:3,892 opening theaters/3,892 max. theaters, 11.8 weeks average run per theater
